questions.prompt: Before relying on an exterior-shell code citation, what should you verify first?
choices: That the code edition and amendment status are currently adopted in Oregon
That the citation appears in a prior estimate template
That another contractor used the same citation last year
That the section has the longest paragraph
questions.explanation: BCD's adopted-codes listing is the official place to verify current editions and interim amendments.

questions.prompt: Which chapter supplies Oregon's statewide statutory framework for building code administration?
questions.explanation: ORS Chapter 455 is Oregon's statewide building-code administration framework.

questions.prompt: Which workflow best reduces exterior-shell compliance errors in scenario questions?
choices: Identify scope, verify active code edition, check amendment timing, then confirm permit path
Estimate labor first and skip code checks until inspection
Use only old project references to save lookup time
Assume no permit implications for exterior-envelope work
questions.explanation: A structured authority-and-edition workflow helps avoid outdated-code and permit-assumption mistakes.
